input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-05T01:01:31.663Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to provide values for `Light`, `Dark`, and optionally `Default`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

When the device switches between light and dark mode, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically — no event handling needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (convenience method)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property type
myButton.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concept | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reactive** | Bindings update live when the user toggles the system theme |
| **`Default`** | Optional third value used when the OS theme is unspecified |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme, or `AppTheme.Unspecified` to follow the system |
| **Resources** | For reuse, define colors in a `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `StaticResource` inside `AppThemeBinding` |

**Tip — combine with resource dictionaries** for maintainability:

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml -->
<Color x:Key="PageBg_Light">#FFFFFF</Color>
<Color x:Key="PageBg_Dark">#1E1E1E</Color>

<!-- In your page -->
BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light={StaticResource PageBg_Light},
                                  Dark={StaticResource PageBg_Dark}}"
```

This keeps all theme colors in one place instead of scattered across pages.
